verses.latin_vulgate: Verba Congregantis, filii Vomentis. Visio quam locutus est vir cum quo est Deus, et qui Deo secum morante confortatus, ait :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְרֵ֤י ׀ אָג֥וּר בִּן־יָקֶ֗ה הַמַּ֫שָּׂ֥א נְאֻ֣ם הַ֭גֶּבֶר לְאִיתִיאֵ֑ל לְאִ֖יתִיאֵ֣ל וְאֻכָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: The words of Gatherer the son of Vomiter. The vision which the man spoke, with whom God is, and who being strengthened by God, abiding with him, said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Agurin Jakenin pojan pojan sanat ja opetus, jonka se mies pujui Itielille, Itielille ja Ukkalille.
verses.king_james: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, even the prophecy: the man spake unto Ithiel, even unto Ithiel and Ucal,

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultissimus sum virorum,  et sapientia hominum non est mecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י בַ֣עַר אָנֹכִ֣י מֵאִ֑ישׁ וְלֹֽא־בִינַ֖ת אָדָ֣ם לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I am the most foolish of men, and the wisdom of men is not with me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen kaikkein hulluin, ja ihmisten ymmärrys ei ole minussa.
verses.king_james: Surely I am more brutish than any man, and have not the understanding of a man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non didici sapientiam,  et non novi scientiam sanctorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹֽא־לָמַ֥דְתִּי חׇכְמָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת קְדֹשִׁ֣ים אֵדָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have not learned wisdom, and have not known the science of saints.
verses.finnish_biblia: En minä ole oppinut viisautta, ja en tiedä pyhäin taitoa.
verses.king_james: I neither learned wisdom, nor have the knowledge of the holy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis ascendit in cælum, atque descendit ?  quis continuit spiritum in manibus suis ?  quis colligavit aquas quasi in vestimento ?  quis suscitavit omnes terminos terræ ?  quod nomen est ejus, et quod nomen filii ejus, si nosti ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֤י עָלָֽה־שָׁמַ֨יִם ׀ וַיֵּרַ֡ד מִ֤י אָֽסַף־ר֨וּחַ ׀ בְּחׇפְנָ֡יו מִ֤י צָֽרַר־מַ֨יִם ׀ בַּשִּׂמְלָ֗ה מִ֭י הֵקִ֣ים כׇּל־אַפְסֵי־אָ֑רֶץ מַה־שְּׁמ֥וֹ וּמַֽה־שֶּׁם־בְּ֝נ֗וֹ כִּ֣י תֵדָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who hath ascended up into heaven, and descended? who hath held the wind in his hands? who hath bound up the waters together as in a garment? who hath raised up all the borders of the earth? what is his name, and what is the name of his son, if thou knowest?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka taivaaseen menee ylös ja tulee alas? kuka käsittää tuulen pivoonsa? kuka sitoo veden vaatteeseen? kuka 0n asttanut kaikki maan ääret? mikä on hänen nimensä? kuinka hänen poikansa kutsutaan? tiedätkös?
verses.king_james: Who hath ascended up into heaven, or descended? who hath gathered the wind in his fists? who hath bound the waters in a garment? who hath established all the ends of the earth? what is his name, and what is his son’s name, if thou canst tell?

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis sermo Dei ignitus :  clypeus est sperantibus in se.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־אִמְרַ֣ת אֱל֣וֹהַּ צְרוּפָ֑ה מָגֵ֥ן ה֝֗וּא לַחֹסִ֥ים בּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Every word of God is fire tried: he is a buckler to them that hope in him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki Jumalan sanat ovat kirkastetut, ja ovat kilpi niille, jotka uskovat hänen päällensä.
verses.king_james: Every word of God is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne addas quidquam verbis illius,  et arguaris, inveniarisque mendax.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תּ֥וֹסְףְּ עַל־דְּבָרָ֑יו פֶּן־יוֹכִ֖יחַ בְּךָ֣ וְנִכְזָֽבְתָּ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Add not any thing to his words, lest thou be reproved and found a liar:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä lisää hänen sanoihinsa, ettei hän sinua rankaisisi, ja sinä löyttäisiin valehteliaksi.
verses.king_james: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo rogavi te :  ne deneges mihi antequam moriar :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁ֭תַּיִם שָׁאַ֣לְתִּי מֵאִתָּ֑ךְ אַל־תִּמְנַ֥ע מִ֝מֶּ֗נִּי בְּטֶ֣רֶם אָמֽוּת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Two things I have asked of thee, deny them not to me before I die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kahta minä sinulta anon, ettes minulta niitä kieltäisi, ennenkuin minä kuolen:
verses.king_james: Two things have I required of thee; deny me them not before I die:

verses.latin_vulgate: vanitatem et verba mendacia longe fac a me ;  mendicitatem et divitias ne dederis mihi :  tribue tantum victui meo necessaria,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁ֤וְא ׀ וּֽדְבַר־כָּזָ֡ב הַרְחֵ֬ק מִמֶּ֗נִּי רֵ֣אשׁ וָ֭עֹשֶׁר אַל־תִּֽתֶּן־לִ֑י הַ֝טְרִיפֵ֗נִי לֶ֣חֶם חֻקִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Remove far from me vanity, and lying words. Give me neither beggary, nor riches: give me only the necessaries of life:
verses.finnish_biblia: Väärä oppi ja valhe olkoon minusta kaukana: köyhyyttä ja rikkautta älä minulle anna; vaan anna minun saada määrätty osani ravinnosta.
verses.king_james: Remove far from me vanity and lies: give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me:

verses.latin_vulgate: ne forte satiatus illiciar ad negandum,  et dicam : Quis est Dominus ?  aut egestate compulsus, furer,  et perjurem nomen Dei mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּ֥ן־אֶשְׂבַּ֨ע ׀ וְכִחַשְׁתִּי֮ וְאָמַ֗רְתִּי מִ֥י־יְ֫הֹוָ֥ה וּפֶֽן־אִוָּרֵ֥שׁ וְגָנַ֑בְתִּי וְ֝תָפַ֗שְׂתִּי שֵׁ֣ם אֱלֹהָֽי׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Lest perhaps being filled, I should be tempted to deny, and say: Who is the Lord? or being compelled by poverty, I should steal, and forswear the name of my God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Etten minä, jos minä ylen ravituksi tulisin, kieltäisi sinua ja sanoisi: kuka on Herra? eli, jos minä ylen köyhäksi tulisin, varastaisi, ja syntiä tekisi Jumalani nimeä vastaan.
verses.king_james: Lest I be full, and deny thee, and say, Who is the Lord? or lest I be poor, and steal, and take the name of my God in vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne accuses servum ad dominum suum,  ne forte maledicat tibi, et corruas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּלְשֵׁ֣ן עֶ֭בֶד אֶל־אֲדֹנָ֑ו פֶּֽן־יְקַלֶּלְךָ֥ וְאָשָֽׁמְתָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Accuse not a servant to his master, lest he curse thee, and thou fall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä kanna palvelian päälle hänen isäntänsä edessä, ettei hän sinua kiroilisi ja sinä niin nuhteeseen tulet.
verses.king_james: Accuse not a servant unto his master, lest he curse thee, and thou be found guilty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Generatio quæ patri suo maledicit,  et quæ matri suæ non benedicit ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּ֭וֹר אָבִ֣יו יְקַלֵּ֑ל וְאֶת־אִ֝מּ֗וֹ לֹ֣א יְבָרֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother.
verses.finnish_biblia: On niitä, jotka kiroilevat isäänsä ja ei siunaa äitiänsä.
verses.king_james: There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother.

verses.latin_vulgate: generatio quæ sibi munda videtur,  et tamen non est lota a sordibus suis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּ֭וֹר טָה֣וֹר בְּעֵינָ֑יו וּ֝מִצֹּאָת֗וֹ לֹ֣א רֻחָֽץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A generation that are pure in their own eyes, and yet are not washed from their filthiness.
verses.finnish_biblia: On niitäkin, jotka luulevat itsensä puhtaaksi, ja ei ole kuitenkaan saastaisuudestansa pestyt.
verses.king_james: There is a generation that are pure in their own eyes, and yet is not washed from their filthiness.

verses.latin_vulgate: generatio cujus excelsi sunt oculi,  et palpebræ ejus in alta surrectæ ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּ֭וֹר מָה־רָמ֣וּ עֵינָ֑יו וְ֝עַפְעַפָּ֗יו יִנָּשֵֽׂאוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A generation, whose eyes are lofty, and their eyelids lifted up on high.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ovat myös, jotka silmänsä nostavat, ja silmälautansa korottavat;
verses.king_james: There is a generation, O how lofty are their eyes! and their eyelids are lifted up.

verses.latin_vulgate: generatio quæ pro dentibus gladios habet,  et commandit molaribus suis,  ut comedat inopes de terra,  et pauperes ex hominibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּ֤וֹר ׀ חֲרָב֣וֹת שִׁנָּיו֮ וּֽמַאֲכָל֢וֹת מְֽתַלְּעֹ֫תָ֥יו לֶאֱכֹ֣ל עֲנִיִּ֣ים מֵאֶ֑רֶץ וְ֝אֶבְיוֹנִ֗ים מֵאָדָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: A generation that for teeth hath swords, and grindeth with their jaw teeth, to devour the needy from off the earth, and the poor from among men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ovat, joilla on miekka hammasten siassa, ja veitset heidän syömähampainansa, ja syövät vaivaiset maan päältä, ja köyhät ihmisten seasta.
verses.king_james: There is a generation, whose teeth are as swords, and their jaw teeth as knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, and the needy from among men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sanguisugæ duæ sunt filiæ,  dicentes : Affer, affer.  Tria sunt insaturabilia,  et quartum quod numquam dicit : Sufficit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַ֥עֲלוּקָ֨ה ׀ שְׁתֵּ֥י בָנוֹת֮ הַ֤ב ׀ הַ֥֫ב שָׁל֣וֹשׁ הֵ֭נָּה לֹ֣א תִשְׂבַּ֑עְנָה אַ֝רְבַּ֗ע לֹא־אָ֥מְרוּ הֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: The horseleech hath two daughters that say: Bring, bring. There are three things that never are satisfied, and the fourth never saith: It is enough.
verses.finnish_biblia: Verenimijällä on kaksi tytärtä: tuo tänne, tuo tänne. Kolme on tyytymätöintä, ja tosin neljä ei sano kyllä olevan:
verses.king_james: The horseleach hath two daughters, crying, Give, give. There are three things that are never satisfied, yea, four things say not, It is enough:

verses.latin_vulgate: Infernus, et os vulvæ,  et terra quæ non satiatur aqua :  ignis vero numquam dicit : Sufficit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁאוֹל֮ וְעֹ֢צֶ֫ר רָ֥חַם אֶ֭רֶץ לֹא־שָׂ֣בְעָה מַּ֑יִם וְ֝אֵ֗שׁ לֹא־אָ֥מְרָה הֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hell and the mouth of the womb, and the earth which is not satisfied with water: and the fire never saith: It is enough.
verses.finnish_biblia: Helvetti, vaimon suljettu kohtu, maa, joka ei vedellä täytetä: ja tuli ei sano: jo kyllä on.
verses.king_james: The grave; and the barren womb; the earth that is not filled with water; and the fire that saith not, It is enough.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculum qui subsannat patrem,  et qui despicit partum matris suæ,  effodiant eum corvi de torrentibus,  et comedant eum filii aquilæ !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֤יִן ׀ תִּ֥לְעַ֣ג לְאָב֮ וְתָבֻ֢ז לִֽיקְּהַ֫ת־אֵ֥ם יִקְּר֥וּהָ עֹֽרְבֵי־נַ֑חַל וְֽיֹאכְל֥וּהָ בְנֵי־נָֽשֶׁר׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The eye that mocketh at his father, and that despiseth the labour of his mother in bearing him, let the ravens of the brooks pick it out, and the young eagles eat it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silmä joka häpäisee isäänsä, ja katsoo ylön totella äitiänsä, sen kaarneet ojan tykönä hakkaavat ulos, ja kotkan pojat syövät.
verses.king_james: The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tria sunt difficilia mihi,  et quartum penitus ignoro :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה הֵ֭מָּה נִפְלְא֣וּ מִמֶּ֑נִּי (וארבע) [וְ֝אַרְבָּעָ֗ה] לֹ֣א יְדַעְתִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Three things are hard to me, and the fourth I am utterly ignorant of.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kolme minulle ovat ihmeelliset, ja neljää en minä tiedä:
verses.king_james: There be three things which are too wonderful for me, yea, four which I know not:

verses.latin_vulgate: viam aquilæ in cælo,  viam colubri super petram,  viam navis in medio mari,  et viam viri in adolescentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דֶּ֤רֶךְ הַנֶּ֨שֶׁר ׀ בַּשָּׁמַיִם֮ דֶּ֥רֶךְ נָחָ֗שׁ עֲלֵ֫י־צ֥וּר דֶּרֶךְ־אֳנִיָּ֥ה בְלֶב־יָ֑ם וְדֶ֖רֶךְ גֶּ֣בֶר בְּעַלְמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: There are three things, which go well, and the fourth that walketh happily:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kotkan tiet taivaan alla, käärmeen retket kalliolla, haahden jäljet meren keskellä, ja miehen jäljet piian tykö.
verses.king_james: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent upon a rock; the way of a ship in the midst of the sea; and the way of a man with a maid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Talis est et via mulieris adulteræ,  quæ comedit, et tergens os suum  dicit : Non sum operata malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֤ן ׀ דֶּ֥רֶךְ אִשָּׁ֗ה מְנָ֫אָ֥פֶת אָ֭כְלָה וּמָ֣חֲתָה פִ֑יהָ וְ֝אָמְרָ֗ה לֹא־פָעַ֥לְתִּי אָֽוֶן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Such also is the way of an adulterous woman, who eateth and wipeth her mouth, and saith: I have done no evil.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin on myös porton polut: hän syö ja pyyhkii suunsa, ja sanoo: en minä mitään pahaa ole tehnyt.
verses.king_james: Such is the way of an adulterous woman; she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per tria movetur terra,  et quartum non potest sustinere :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֣חַת שָׁ֭לוֹשׁ רָ֣גְזָה אֶ֑רֶץ וְתַ֥חַת אַ֝רְבַּ֗ע לֹא־תוּכַ֥ל שְׂאֵֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: By three things the earth is disturbed, and the fourth it cannot bear.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kolmen kautta maakunta tulee levottomuuteen, ja tosin neljää hän ei voi kärsiä:
verses.king_james: For three things the earth is disquieted, and for four which it cannot bear:

verses.latin_vulgate: per servum, cum regnaverit ;  per stultum, cum saturatus fuerit cibo ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּֽחַת־עֶ֭בֶד כִּ֣י יִמְל֑וֹךְ וְ֝נָבָ֗ל כִּ֣י יִֽשְׂבַּֽע־לָֽחֶם׃
verses.douay_rheims: By a slave when he reigneth: by a fool when be is filled with meat:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin palvelia rupee hallitsemaan, kuin hullu ylen ravituksi tulee,
verses.king_james: For a servant when he reigneth; and a fool when he is filled with meat;

verses.latin_vulgate: per odiosam mulierem, cum in matrimonio fuerit assumpta ;  et per ancillam, cum fuerit hæres dominæ suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֣חַת שְׂ֭נוּאָה כִּ֣י תִבָּעֵ֑ל וְ֝שִׁפְחָ֗ה כִּֽי־תִירַ֥שׁ גְּבִרְתָּֽהּ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: By an odious woman when she is married: and by a bondwoman when she is heir to her mistress.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin ilkiä naitetaan, ja kuin piika tulee emäntänsä perilliseksi.
verses.king_james: For an odious woman when she is married; and an handmaid that is heir to her mistress.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quatuor sunt minima terræ,  et ipsa sunt sapientiora sapientibus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַרְבָּ֣עָה הֵ֭ם קְטַנֵּי־אָ֑רֶץ וְ֝הֵ֗מָּה חֲכָמִ֥ים מְחֻכָּמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: There are four very little things of the earth, and they are wiser than the wise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Neljä on pientä maan päällä, ja ovat toimellisemmat viisaita.
verses.king_james: There be four things which are little upon the earth, but they are exceeding wise:

verses.latin_vulgate: formicæ, populus infirmus,  qui præparat in messe cibum sibi ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭נְּמָלִים עַ֣ם לֹא־עָ֑ז וַיָּכִ֖ינוּ בַקַּ֣יִץ לַחְמָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The ants, a feeble people, which provide themselves food in the harvest:
verses.finnish_biblia: Myyriäiset, heikko väki, jotka kuitenkin elatuksensa suvella toimittavat;
verses.king_james: The ants are a people not strong, yet they prepare their meat in the summer;

verses.latin_vulgate: lepusculus, plebs invalida,  qui collocat in petra cubile suum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁ֭פַנִּים עַ֣ם לֹא־עָצ֑וּם וַיָּשִׂ֖ימוּ בַסֶּ֣לַע בֵּיתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The rabbit, a weak people, which maketh its bed in the rock:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaninit, heikko väki, jotka kuitenkin pesänsä kalliolle tekevät;
verses.king_james: The conies are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks;

verses.latin_vulgate: regem locusta non habet,  et egreditur universa per turmas suas ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֶ֭לֶךְ אֵ֣ין לָאַרְבֶּ֑ה וַיֵּצֵ֖א חֹצֵ֣ץ כֻּלּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The locust hath no king, yet they all go out by their bands:
verses.finnish_biblia: Heinäsirkoilla ei ole kuningasta, kuitenkin he lähtevät kaikki ylös joukossansa;
verses.king_james: The locusts have no king, yet go they forth all of them by bands;

verses.latin_vulgate: stellio manibus nititur,  et moratur in ædibus regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂ֭מָמִית בְּיָדַ֣יִם תְּתַפֵּ֑שׂ וְ֝הִ֗יא בְּהֵ֣יכְלֵי מֶֽלֶךְ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The stellio supporteth itself on hands, and dwelleth in kings' houses.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hämähäkki kehrää käsillänsä, ja on kuninkaan linnoissa.
verses.king_james: The spider taketh hold with her hands, and is in kings’ palaces.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tria sunt quæ bene gradiuntur,  et quartum quod incedit feliciter :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה הֵ֭מָּה מֵיטִ֣יבֵי צָ֑עַד וְ֝אַרְבָּעָ֗ה מֵיטִ֥בֵי לָֽכֶת׃
verses.finnish_biblia: Kolmella on jalo käyntö, ja tosin neljä astuu hyvin:
verses.king_james: There be three things which go well, yea, four are comely in going:

verses.latin_vulgate: leo, fortissimus bestiarum,  ad nullius pavebit occursum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַ֭יִשׁ גִּבּ֣וֹר בַּבְּהֵמָ֑ה וְלֹא־יָ֝שׁ֗וּב מִפְּנֵי־כֹֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: A lion, the strongest of beasts, who hath no fear of any thing he meeteth:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jalopeura, voimallinen petoin seassa, joka ei palaja kenenkään edestä;
verses.king_james: A lion which is strongest among beasts, and turneth not away for any;

verses.latin_vulgate: gallus succinctus lumbos ;  et aries ; nec est rex, qui resistat ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זַרְזִ֣יר מׇתְנַ֣יִם אוֹ־תָ֑יִשׁ וּ֝מֶ֗לֶךְ אַלְק֥וּם עִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: A cock girded about the loins: and a ram: and a king, whom none can resist.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hurtta, jolla vahvat sivut ovat, ja kauris, ja kuningas, jota vastaan ei kenkään tohdi.
verses.king_james: A greyhound; an he goat also; and a king, against whom there is no rising up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est qui stultus apparuit postquam elevatus est in sublime ;  si enim intellexisset, ori suo imposuisset manum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־נָבַ֥לְתָּ בְהִתְנַשֵּׂ֑א וְאִם־זַ֝מּ֗וֹתָ יָ֣ד לְפֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: There is that hath appeared a fool after he was lifted up on high: for if he had understood, he would have laid his hand upon his mouth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos sinä olet tyhmästi tehnyt, ja korottanut sinuas, ja jos sinä olet jotakin pahaa ajatellut, niin laske käsi suus päälle.
verses.king_james: If thou hast done foolishly in lifting up thyself, or if thou hast thought evil, lay thine hand upon thy mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem fortiter premit ubera ad eliciendum lac exprimit butyrum ;  et qui vehementer emungit elicit sanguinem ;  et qui provocat iras producit discordias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י מִ֪יץ חָלָ֡ב י֘וֹצִ֤יא חֶמְאָ֗ה וּֽמִיץ־אַ֭ף י֣וֹצִיא דָ֑ם וּמִ֥יץ אַ֝פַּ֗יִם י֣וֹצִיא רִֽיב׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he that strongly squeezeth the paps to bring out milk, straineth out butter: and he that violently bloweth his nose, bringeth out blood: and he that provoketh wrath, bringeth forth strife.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka rieskaa kirnuu, hän tekee voita: ja jokaa nenää pusertaa, hän vaatii ulos veren: ja joka vihaa kehoittaa, hän vaatii riitaan.
verses.king_james: Surely the churning of milk bringeth forth butter, and the wringing of the nose bringeth forth blood: so the forcing of wrath bringeth forth strife.
